title: Deliverable
description: The DELIVERABLE contract — schema-bound work unit per DLP-STRATEGY. A deliverable is the canonical unit of work: declared scope, acceptance criteria, authority, lifecycle state, and audit trail. Replaces prose-in-markdown deliverable specs. Agent enters at S4-authored, transitions through S5-in-progress and S6-staged, terminates at S7-merged or S8-closed (or reverted). Instance files live at schemas/_instances/deliverables/.

id.description: Stable identifier. Convention: d-YYYY-MM-DD-slug. Matches the pattern used in follow-up-register relatedDecisions and throughout the portfolio.

version.description: Semver of the deliverable spec itself. MAJOR: scope or acceptance criteria change. MINOR: added output or refinement. PATCH: clarification, no behavior change.

description.description: What this deliverable accomplishes — 2-3 sentences capturing intent and substantive scope.

authority.description: Governance authority block. Currently free-form strings; will reference authority-tier.enum.json (5-tier authorization model — strategy deferred) when that schema is promoted. Conventional values include 'Claude (chat), L1', 'Erik Luhtala (L4)', or descriptive role strings.

approver.description: Who holds approval authority at each gate (typically Erik L4 in current portfolio).

revertAuthority.description: Who can revert or close the deliverable.

lifecycleState.description: DLP-STRATEGY lifecycle state. S4 = spec authored, awaiting approval to begin. S5 = execution underway. S6 = work staged for merge. S7 = merged to main. S8 = closed (post-merge follow-ups complete). reverted = rolled back.

scope.description: Scope envelope. Branch and scopeFiles together define exactly what work this deliverable is authorized to touch.

branch.description: Feature branch name. Type prefix from commit-conventions; dated suffix YYYY-MM-DD. Memory rule #18.

scopeFiles.description: Files or globs this deliverable is authorized to touch. Enables F7 amendment-substrate-recheck rule to compute overlap with concurrent deliverables.

outputs.description: Named outputs (OUT-N). Each must have acceptance criteria.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Concrete, verifiable criteria — each one a pass/fail check.

validation.description: Validation steps. preMerge must all pass before merge gate; postMerge runs after to verify the landing.

preMerge.description: Pre-merge validation steps. Examples: 'ajv compile passes', 'refs rebuild --check exits 0', 'all OUT acceptance criteria met'.

postMerge.description: Post-merge verification steps. Examples: 'main shows X registered', 'consumer Y validates against new schema'.

transitions.description: Lifecycle state changes. Reuses refinementRecord pattern from rule.schema.json — git holds the proof; schema holds logical state.

from.description: Prior lifecycle state. Empty string for initial S4-authored transition.

date.description: Wall-clock date of the transition (system time at authoring).

commitSha.description: Commit SHA marking the transition. Reuses follow-up-register resolution.commitSha naming.

prUrl.description: PR URL marking the transition. Reuses follow-up-register resolution.prUrl naming.

trigger.description: What fired the transition (e.g., 'L4 approval', 'merge', 'acceptance criteria met').

parentDeliverable.description: Parent deliverable id if this is a child of a multi-deliverable arc.

childDeliverables.description: Child deliverable ids. Inverse of parentDeliverable.

relatedFollowUps.description: Follow-up ids opened, closed, or referenced by this deliverable. Pattern matches follow-up-register canonical id form.

spikeOutcome.description: Only present if this deliverable terminates as a SPIKE per memory rule #17. Names the terminal outcome.

prUrl.description: Link to the PR landing the deliverable.

mergeCommitSha.description: Squash-merge commit SHA on main. Populated on transition to S7-merged.

notes.description: Free-form context not captured structurally. Rationale-class field — prose-inside-instance is the legitimate home for prose per the no-md-terminus principle.
